Impact of Infrastructure & Socioeconomic Status on Rural Education Independent Research | Primary Investigator Objective: Investigated how physical infrastructure and socioeconomic status (SES) influence educational outcomes in rural Indian communities. Data Analysis: Analyzed ASER data indicating that while 96.7% of children are enrolled in schools, basic infrastructure remains a barrier, with only 61.75% of schools having accessible toilets and 11% offering transportation. Key Findings: Identified that distance to schools significantly impacts attendance, particularly for girls, and that lower SES correlates with higher dropout rates due to financial constraints. Technical Insights: Utilized Python (Seaborn) to visualize the "Digital Divide," highlighting that while platforms like DIKSHA exist, fewer than 20% of rural schools have functional internet access. Actionable Recommendations: Proposed a multi-faceted approach involving government infrastructure investment, community-based digital learning centers, and subsidized transportation.
